steps.title: Pinpoint the profit claim
steps.content: Ortega and Solis claim rural borrowers sell less frequently as a strategic choice that increases profit per unit sold. The supporting finding must explain how fewer sales trips could lower per-unit costs or raise per-unit returns.

steps.title: Apply the fixed-cost logic
steps.content: If bringing goods to market has a high cost that is mostly the same no matter how many goods are brought, rural sellers can wait and bring more goods at once. Spreading the fixed trip cost across more units raises profit per unit.

steps.title: Reject weaker findings
steps.content: A describes a fee tied to each day's proceeds, not a benefit from selling less often. B concerns selling location and competition, not frequency. C concerns initial loan size, not profit per unit sold. The answer is D.
